desc: Cesky Krumlov is one of Europe's most photogenic old towns, but the real trick is staying overnight rather than treating it as a quick coach-stop: castle courtyards and tower views above the Vltava loop, a genuinely rare Baroque theatre, riverside rafting in warm months and quiet lanes once the late-morning day-trip crush fades. It rewards early starts and evening wandering far more than it rewards ticking off the postcard angle at noon.

todo.name: Cesky Krumlov Castle
todo.desc: Climb through the castle complex for town views and a sense of why the Vltava bend is so dramatic.

todo.name: Castle theatre tour
todo.desc: Book ahead for the rare Baroque theatre interiors if the seasonal schedule fits.

todo.name: Vltava rafting
todo.desc: Float through town in warmer months for a playful view of the old roofs and castle tower.

todo.name: Egon Schiele Art Centrum
todo.desc: Balance medieval streets with modern art and local cultural context.

todo.name: Castle tower and gardens
todo.desc: Climb for the essential panorama, then use the gardens to escape the tightest old-town lanes for a while.

todo.name: Latran and evening old town
todo.desc: Walk the quieter streets after the day-trippers leave, when the town finally feels like a place rather than a queue.

wifi.note: Good across most hotels, pensions and cafes; expect occasional drops in older stone buildings and don't rely on perfect coverage in castle grounds.

prac.fltNote: Cesky Krumlov has no airport and no destination-level LGW/LCY nonstop routing. Prague is usually the easiest UK gateway thanks to direct London links including LGW service, while Linz is closer but more limited and Vienna offers more flight choice with a longer onward transfer. The town's bigger friction is not the flight itself but the final rail, bus or road leg and the late-morning day-trip crowding.
prac.lang: Czech; English is common in main visitor areas
prac.visa: Schengen rules apply; British citizens can normally visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period and should also note the phased rollout of the EU Entry/Exit System from 10 April 2026.
bestFor: One-night escapes from Prague or Austria
alerts: Day-trip groups crowd the core from late morning to mid-afternoon, so do headline viewpoints early or stay overnight.
Baroque theatre and some castle interiors need advance booking and have limited access because of conservation needs.
River activities are seasonal and water-level dependent.
Cobblestones, slopes and the walk from the station make light luggage far more pleasant than a big case.

sweet: May, June and September are the best balance for castle access, river activity and lighter crowd pressure than the height of summer, while December suits festive atmosphere more than outdoor exploring.
